user_system_path: Seam #421 — owner direction: '15 min is too sporadic; right away; I don't want to pay [for Pro].' Replace the time-based cron plan with event-driven immediate triggering. The mechanism already exists: sync-now (Google/Microsoft) chains maybeRunWorkdayPresenceTriggerRunnerForUser, which is FREE (repackages the already-paid daily artifact into a reviewable move; a DB read, no LLM), and sync-now is server-side rate-limited (429). This wires the dashboard Today panel to fire a throttled background sync-now on app open, then re-runs loadCard() so the current move surfaces immediately — no cron, no Pro, no per-open paid generation. Constraint: NO paid API calls to test — prove in the harness.
allowed_files_raw: Seam #421: one guarded effect in app/dashboard/page.tsx (TodayPanel) + control-plane.
forbidden_files_raw: No schema, no deps, no runtime/API logic, no lib changes — reuse the existing sync-now + trigger path.
required_local_proof: npm run gate:continuity && npm run build && npm run lint
required_browser_proof: Build + lint + tsc clean. Live owner validation: open the dashboard Today panel and confirm it auto-syncs and surfaces the current move without a manual sync (throttled to 5 min/session).
acceptance_condition: Opening the dashboard fires a throttled background sync-now for connected providers and refreshes the Right Now card so the current move appears immediately, with no new cron, no paid generation on the path, and gate:continuity + build + lint green.
anti_regression_checks: no paid LLM on the open path (trigger runner repackages the already-paid artifact)
client throttle (5 min) on top of the route 429 rate limit
stop_condition: Stop when the draft PR for #421 is open with CI green, or BLOCKED_WITH_EXACT_RECEIPT on owner-side dashboard validation.
next_command: Owner: open the dashboard and confirm it auto-syncs + surfaces the current move. Then the daily generation must actually produce an artifact (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) for the trigger to surface — confirm that env. Hygiene backlog captured in #420.
